sections.paragraph:
Ἰωσῆς, ῆ or ῆτος (s. Mayser 274), ὁ (the name is found IG XIV [Sic. It. 949] Κλαύδιος Ἰωσῆς; ins fr. Cyrene: Sb 1742. S. ibid. 3757 [I a.d.]; BGU 715 I, 4 [CPJ II, 428; s. Schürer III 55] Ἰ. ὁ καὶ Τεύφιλος; Jos., Bell. 4, 66 v.l. S. B-D-F §53, 2; 55, 2; Rob. 263; Wuthnow 60) Joses

① name of a brother of Jesus Mk 6:3; Mt 13:55 v.l.; s. Ἰωσήφ 5.

② son of a Mary and brother of James the younger Mk 15:40, 47; Mt 27:56 v.l. (for Ἰωσήφ, q.v. 9; on the text s. AMerx, D. Vier kanon. Ev. II 1, 1902, 430ff).

③ name of a member of the early church better known as Barnabas Ac 4:36 v.l. (s. Ἰωσήφ 7).

④ S. Ἰησοῦς 2.—M-M.

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

It. It. = Itala, early version of the NT in Latin

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

Cyrene Cyrene = text fr. Cyrene

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

CPJ CPJ = Corpus Papyrorum Judaicarum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Wuthnow Wuthnow = HW., Die semitischen Menschennamen—List 6

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 486
